shot_notes: The silence is broken after three days
evidence_summary: Establish the Wizard's transition from a powerful figure to a desperate fraud while presenting the balloon journey as the new objective. The scale of the throne room must emphasize the isolation and weight of his secret.

After three days of silence, Oz summons Dorothy. He proposes a journey via balloon to cross the desert but reveals his secret identity as a former circus performer. He expresses a desperate need to flee before his fraud is discovered.
From suspenseful waiting to revelation and shared desperation.
Dorothy stands alone in the vast silence of the throne room after three days of waiting.
Oz and Dorothy are united by a shared, desperate plan to flee.
The Wizard breaks his silence to propose an escape and confess his true nature.
